paragraphs: Every family has a story worth knowing—especially the gods.
When Chaos gave birth to The Primordials, those unstoppable forces created the seeds of life.

From them, the trees of life sprouted. Then the Cardinal gods emerged from the cosmic canopy. They combined their powers to create the immortal spirits and instructed them to construct a realm where the physical and non-physical could coexist—The Realm of Paradise.

The Cardinal gods, immortal spirits, and physical beings lived in harmony until trouble tore through paradise. Disgusted, the Cardinal gods created a dimension below to govern the unjust souls; they called the dimension the Realm of the Dead. Then, the God of Destiny and the God of Fate were born and gave birth to my parents: The God of Time and The God of Space.

Together, they sculpted the first mortals and watched them navigate their pilgrimage to repent for their treachery. Upon death, those forgiven returned to The Realm of Paradise, but the ones who failed to redeem themselves were reincarnated in The Realm of the Dead without memory.

When the first mortal world began to overflow with disrespect and malice, the Cardinal gods incarnated to provide guidance; one in particular was betrayed. As a result, The God of War was born and decreed all civilizations in breach of cosmic law in The Realm of the Dead shall be sentenced to a harrowing judgment to determine if the world was worth salvaging or if it needed to be purged. The Judgment became a horrific event for mortals and a cathartic game for the divine, providing entertainment throughout dimensions.

To watch The Judgment, the Cardinal gods created a realm above Paradise called Utopia—an always-changing kingdom made of metal and gemstone; a life-giving dimension, with glittering sand and oceans that churn liquid moonstone. Tall mountains made of luminous moldavite pierce the horizon, and trees with twisty branches made of layers of copper and iron hum, gifting gold leaves and flowers made of diamond to green grass as fragile as glass.
Come meet the deities who test fragile families in strife against the odds.
For every family has a story—especially a family of mortals who become gods.
